---
title: "Assignment 1: Multiple Linear Regression with Indicator (Dummy) Variables"
author: "Omkar Navle | Roll No: A037 | SAP ID: 86062500050"
date: "2026-03-24"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

## Dataset: Hotel Booking Demand | Source: Kaggle
Dataset link: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/jessemostipak/hotel-booking-demand

```{r load-library}
# LOAD LIBRARY
library(readr)
```

```{r load-data}
# LOAD DATA
# Update this path to where you saved the CSV file
df <- read_csv("hotel_bookings.csv")

View(df)
str(df)
```

```{r summary-statistics}
# SUMMARY STATISTICS
summary(df)
```

```{r missing-values}
# CHECK MISSING VALUES
sapply(df, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))

# HANDLE MISSING VALUES
df$children[is.na(df$children)] <- mean(df$children, na.rm = TRUE)
df$country[is.na(df$country)]   <- "Unknown"
df$agent[is.na(df$agent)]       <- 0
df$company[is.na(df$company)]   <- 0

# VERIFY — all zeros
sapply(df, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
```

```{r correlation}
# CORRELATION CHECK (NUMERIC VARS)
cor(df[, c("adr", "lead_time", "stays_in_week_nights")])
```

```{r boxplot}
# BOXPLOT — justifying dummy variable need
boxplot(adr ~ hotel,
        data  = df,
        ylim  = c(0, 500),
        main  = "ADR by Hotel Type",
        col   = c("lightblue", "lightgreen"))
```

```{r create-dummy}
# CREATE DUMMY VARIABLE
# hotel_dummy = 1 if Resort Hotel (treatment), 0 if City Hotel (reference)
df$hotel_dummy <- ifelse(df$hotel == "Resort Hotel", 1, 0)

head(df[, c("hotel", "hotel_dummy", "adr", "lead_time", "stays_in_week_nights")])
```

```{r full-model}
# FULL MODEL
model <- lm(adr ~ lead_time + stays_in_week_nights +
                  previous_bookings_not_canceled + hotel_dummy,
            data = df)

summary(model)
```

```{r refined-model}
# REFINED MODEL (previous_bookings_not_canceled removed)
model_refined <- lm(adr ~ lead_time + stays_in_week_nights + hotel_dummy,
                    data = df)

summary(model_refined)
```
